---
title: "1.3.3 Data compression"
subtitle: "Data Representation P35-P36"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 1 Directory / 返回 Unit 1 目录](../../index.html)

## Data Compression

### Why compression is needed

The calculations in Section <span class="term">1.3.2</span> show that image and sound files can be very large. It is therefore necessary to <span class="term">compress</span> (压缩) files:

::: {.tight-list}
- to <span class="mark">save storage space</span> on devices such as HDDs and SSDs
- to <span class="mark">reduce the time taken to stream</span> a music or video file
- to <span class="mark">reduce the time taken to upload, download or transfer</span> a file across a network
:::

## Data Compression

### Bandwidth and cost

Compressed files contain <span class="mark">fewer bits of data</span> than uncompressed files:

::: {.tight-list}
- this uses less <span class="term">bandwidth</span> (网络带宽), the maximum data transfer rate across a network
- less bandwidth use gives a <span class="mark">faster data transfer rate</span>
- reduced file size can also <span class="mark">reduce costs</span>, for example in cloud storage or paid data transfer
:::

## Classroom Check

### Explain why compression is necessary

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- image and sound files can be <span class="mark">very large</span>
- compression helps <span class="mark">save storage space</span>
- compression helps <span class="mark">reduce upload, download and streaming time</span>
- compressed files use <span class="mark">less bandwidth</span>
- smaller files can <span class="mark">reduce storage or transfer cost</span>
:::

## Bridge

### Next: Lossy and Lossless File Compression

The next deck explains the two main compression approaches and when each is used.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 1 Directory / 返回 Unit 1 目录](../../index.html)
